CLAUDE.mdPacoVK/tapir :: CLAUDE.mdPascal Euhusframework2237Tapir is a Terraform registry implementation (237 stars) built on Quarkus (Java) and React. CLAUDE.md (171 lines) provides architecture, build/dev commands, backend architecture details (layered Quarkus structure, repository pattern, storage/backend abstraction), frontend setup (React/MUI), and important implementation details (API routing workarounds, OIDC auth, test infrastructure with LocalStack/OpenSearch). Strong JVM signal (Quarkus is premier JVM framework). Content is substantial, reusable architectural guidance for building Java microservices with pluggable backends. Depth 2+; JVM-fit high. Author has significant GitHub presence (237 stars on this repo alone).

jstallparttimenerd/jstall :: jstallJohannes Bechbergertool346Depth 3, well-developed methodology for diagnosing any running Java application. High JVM fit: covers thread dumps, deadlock analysis, CPU profiling, flame graphs, GC tuning—core JVM diagnostics. 256 lines of opinionated, reusable guidance with decision tree and scenario-based examples. This is a real skill, not a demo fixture.
📋 yaml
name: Jstall
description: >-
  Comprehensive JVM diagnostics skill with opinionated methodology. Teaches a three-step workflow (discover→diagnose→triage) with a detailed decision tree covering deadlock, high CPU, lock contention, I/O waits, GC pressure, and other JVM failure modes. Includes concrete jstall command examples and expected outputs for each scenario. Deep, reusable guidance authored by repo owner in a formalized MCP skills directory.

jfr-analyzervictorrentea/performance-profiling :: jfr-analyzerVictor Renteatool224Victor Rentea's jfr-analyzer skill provides concrete, opinionated guidance on analyzing Java Flight Recorder (.jfr) data using JDK's native jfr CLI tool. Covers tool usage (summary, print, metadata), JSON parsing, ISO 8601 duration handling, and a structured multi-step analysis workflow (lock contention, GC events, etc.) with practical examples. Strong JVM fit: JFR is core JVM infrastructure. ~140 lines, moderate depth—not entry-level but not exhaustive.

conference-scheduler-timefold-skillstephanj/conference-scheduling-skill :: conference-scheduler-timefold-skillStephan Janssenframework317Substantial, practical JVM skill teaching conference scheduling constraint optimization with TimeFold (Java constraint solver). Prerequisites: Java 21+, Maven. Comprehensive coverage: constraint model taxonomy (speaker conflicts, room conflicts as hard constraints; educational flow, track consistency as soft constraints), multi-day scheduling, CSV input/output formats, solver tuning by conference size (10s-10m time limits). Teaches reusable patterns for OptaPlanner/TimeFold domain modeling. Strong JVM signal: Java 21+ required, Maven build, TimeFold is pure Java. Reusable for any conference scheduling problem.

Authored by speaker, not a demo.
📋 yaml
name: Java Dev
description: >-
  Comprehensive Java 25 development methodology inheriting methodical-dev with Java-specific overrides for plain Java, Quarkus, JBang, and LangChain4j. Structured phases: info gathering (with Java constraints), git verification, planning, implementation, validation. Covers extension-first rule, testing with @QuarkusTest, profile-prefixed config, Gradle/Maven patterns.

java-architectmbellade/ai-dev-toolkit :: java-architectMarco Belladellibest-practices20Genuine, opinionated JVM best practices from a maintainer perspective: Safety (resource leaks, deadlocks, classloader leaks), Concurrency (thread-local storage, event-loop patterns), Code clarity (final params, ASCII-only), Testing (integration/unit balance), Performance (allocation awareness, avoid excessive streams/lambdas in hot paths, prefer method handles over reflection), Documentation (brief Javadoc). Meets depth requirement, high JVM-fit with strong signals, author credibility as Hibernate maintainer, fully transferable to any Java project.
📋 yaml
name: Java Architect
description: >-
  Genuine, opinionated JVM best practices from a maintainer perspective: Safety (resource leaks, deadlocks, classloader leaks), Concurrency (thread-local storage, event-loop patterns), Code clarity (final params, ASCII-only), Testing (integration/unit balance), Performance (allocation awareness, avoid excessive streams/lambdas in hot paths, prefer method handles over reflection), Documentation (brief Javadoc). Meets depth requirement, high JVM-fit with strong signals, author credibility as Hibernate maintainer, fully transferable to any Java project.

jooq-best-practicesJohannesRabauer/java-project-analyser :: jooq-best-practicesJohannes Rabauerframework20Real, depth-2+ JVM skill. jOOQ is a core JVM library. Skill names concrete patterns (MULTISET, MERGE as RIGHT JOIN, recursive CTEs, type-safe mapping), references authoritative knowledge files, and provides immediately actionable rules. Author authored this for their project analyser but patterns transfer to any jOOQ user. No project-doc markers — genuinely reusable expertise on a strong JVM signal (jOOQ 3.20 DSL).
📋 yaml
name: Jooq Best Practices
description: >-
  Comprehensive jOOQ DSL knowledge base (3.20 target, PostgreSQL). Links to 21 detailed knowledge files covering anti-patterns, multiset queries, MERGE/upsert, CTEs, aggregates, type system, transactions, pagination. 22 core rules embedded. Transferable expertise for any JVM project using jOOQ; reusable patterns not tied to java-project-analyser itself.
repo: JohannesRabauer/java-project-analyser
skill_path: ".agents/skills/jooq-best-practices/SKILL.md"

fxgl-ai-pathfindingJohannesRabauer/fxgl-skills :: fxgl-ai-pathfindingJohannes Rabauerframework30High-quality, depth-3 JVM skill. FXGL is a core JVM game framework. Skill teaches pathfinding architecture (grid-based A*), state management (GOAP), and NPC decision-making with 350+ lines of working code. Per Rule 2: this belongs in the FXGL bundle but is individually promotable as 'found' if the bundle is confirmed canonical. Not a demo — genuine reusable patterns.
📋 yaml
name: Fxgl Ai Pathfinding
description: >-
  Comprehensive FXGL AI skill covering A* pathfinding (AStarGrid, AStarPathfinder, AStarMoveComponent), GOAP (Goal-Oriented Action Planning), SenseAI vision/hearing, waypoint patrol, procedural generation. Multiple code examples showing grid setup, path calculation, component attachment, wandering behaviors, and NPC AI patterns.

JVM fit is medium: applies to any game project, not FXGL-specific, but canonically published in FXGL bundle and uses FXGL asset folder conventions. Valuable for game developers navigating copyright.
📋 yaml
name: Fxgl Asset Sourcing Attribution
description: >-
  Legal/compliance guidance for sourcing third-party game assets (sprites, audio, music) with proper licensing. Asset organization into FXGL folders, provenance tracking, attribution records for commercial release compliance.
repo: JohannesRabauer/fxgl-skills
skill_path: "skills/fxgl-asset-sourcing-attribution/SKILL.md"
category: tool

trust: curated

quarkus-langchain4j-scaffoldingeldermoraes/quarkus-agentic-scaffolding :: quarkus-langchain4j-scaffoldingElder Moraesframework20Quarkus + LangChain4j Scaffolding (118 lines) teaches reusable project creation guidance: when to use (scaffold new projects/modules), project layout conventions, starter pom.xml patterns, application.properties structure, and step-by-step setup. This is concrete, opinionated guidance for a JVM stack (Quarkus + LangChain4j = strong JVM signals). Depth 2: covers project structure, tooling, and starter patterns. JVM fit high: Quarkus and LangChain4j are listed JVM signals. Authored by eldermoraes in dedicated scaffolding repo, not a demo/workshop artifact.
📋 yaml
name: Quarkus Langchain4J Scaffolding
description: >-
  Genuine reusable scaffolding skill for Quarkus + LangChain4j stack

📦 Workflow bundles · 3 · general agentic workflows (non-JVM)

Cohesive, dependent workflow suites (e.g. a spec-driven-dev pipeline). Not JVM, but listable under the workflow category if you want them — evaluated as a whole.
BundleRepo · stepsAuthorKind#★Why included (reasoning)Promote
spec-driven-developmentantonarhipov/agentskills
01-spec → 02-criteria → 03-rules → 04-review → 05-tasks → 06-execute
Anton Arhipovworkflow613A tight 6-step spec-driven development pipeline (spec → criteria → rules → review → tasks → execute) authored by Anton Arhipov in his dedicated skills collection (13 stars). Every step explicitly declares its pipeline position and reads the previous step's output (spec/proposal.md → spec/spec.md → spec/criteria.md → spec/rules.md → spec/review.md → spec/tasks.yaml), forming a single indivisible suite. Individual skill files are 79–228 lines with rich, opinionated guidance — EARS acceptance-criteria templates with traceability IDs, RFC-2119 constraint language, a severity-graded review protocol, YAML task-list schema, and a full execution-loop with Checkpoint/Blocker report formats. Per rule 5, a coherent, high-quality workflow bundle is directory-worthy under the workflow category; this one easily meets that bar and is the canonical copy (highest stars, most steps, deepest content) per rule 2.
also copied in: kotlinconf-sdd, midifx, sdd-demo
📋 yaml
name: Spec Driven Development
description: >-
  A tight 6-step spec-driven development pipeline (spec → criteria → rules → review → tasks → execute) authored by Anton Arhipov in his dedicated skills collection (13 stars). Every step explicitly declares its pipeline position and reads the previous step's output (spec/proposal.md → spec/spec.md → spec/criteria.md → spec/rules.md → spec/review.md → spec/tasks.yaml), forming a single indivisible suite. Individual skill files are 79–228 lines with rich, opinionated guidance — EARS acceptance-criteria templates with traceability IDs, RFC-2119 constraint language, a severity-graded review protocol, YAML task-list schema, and a full execution-loop with Checkpoint/Blocker report formats. Per rule 5, a coherent, high-quality workflow bundle is directory-worthy under the workflow category; this one easily meets that bar and is the canonical copy (highest stars, most steps, deepest content) per rule 2.
repo: antonarhipov/agentskills
skill_path: ""
category: workflow
languages:
  - agnostic
trust: curated
author: Anton Arhipov
version: "1.0.0"
last_updated: "2026-06-28"
scope: bundle
skills:
  - "01-spec/SKILL.md"
  - "02-criteria/SKILL.md"
  - "03-rules/SKILL.md"
  - "04-review/SKILL.md"
  - "05-tasks/SKILL.md"
  - "06-execute/SKILL.md"
tags:
  - # TODO
spec-driven-development-workshopantonarhipov/sdd-workshop
01-clarify → 02-analyze → 03-constrain → 04-validate → 05-generate-plan
Anton Arhipovworkflow52A 5-step workshop/teaching variant of the same SDD pipeline (clarify → analyze → constrain → validate → generate-plan), thinner per member (36–83 lines each vs 79–228 lines in the canonical) and hosted in a workshop repo (2 stars vs 13). The pipeline is cohesive — each step reads the previous step's artifact — but this is clearly a teaching/conference variant of the canonical found in agentskills. Per the Devnexus 2026 rule, the canonical multi-step pipeline (agentskills, 6 steps, richer content) is promoted as found; this workshop variant is needs_review at best and should not become a second found listing for the same suite.
also copied in: sdd-workshop
📋 yaml
name: Spec Driven Development Workshop
description: >-
  A 5-step workshop/teaching variant of the same SDD pipeline (clarify → analyze → constrain → validate → generate-plan), thinner per member (36–83 lines each vs 79–228 lines in the canonical) and hosted in a workshop repo (2 stars vs 13). The pipeline is cohesive — each step reads the previous step's artifact — but this is clearly a teaching/conference variant of the canonical found in agentskills. Per the Devnexus 2026 rule, the canonical multi-step pipeline (agentskills, 6 steps, richer content) is promoted as found; this workshop variant is needs_review at best and should not become a second found listing for the same suite.
repo: antonarhipov/sdd-workshop
skill_path: ".claude/skills"
category: workflow
languages:
  - agnostic
trust: curated
author: Anton Arhipov
version: "1.0.0"
last_updated: "2026-06-28"
scope: bundle
skills:

  - ".claude/skills/03-constrain/SKILL.md"

spec-driven-dev-pipelineJohannesRabauer/junie-midi-javafx-app
01-spec → 02-criteria → 03-rules → 04-review → 05-tasks → 06-execute
Johannes Rabauerworkflow60Six numbered SKILL.md files form one end-to-end spec-driven development pipeline (proposal → spec → criteria → rules → review → tasks → execute). Each step reads files produced by its predecessor and makes no sense in isolation — a textbook Rule 4 bundle. Total content is ~1040 lines of substantive, language-agnostic guidance: EARS-based acceptance criteria with B-N → AC-N → RULE-N traceability, an Ecosystem Survey gate for technology decisions, a severity-tagged cross-document review step that produces a PASS/FAIL verdict gating the task step, YAML task output with dependency graph and checkpoints, and a checkpoint-halting execution loop. No JVM-specific content, but the Rule 5 escape applies — this is a coherent, high-quality workflow bundle, not a thin single step. The host repo (junie-midi-javafx-app, 0 stars) is an app project, but Rule 1 bars that as a reject signal; the skills make zero reference to JavaFX or MIDI and are fully reusable.
📋 yaml
name: Spec Driven Dev Pipeline
description: >-
  Six numbered SKILL.md files form one end-to-end spec-driven development pipeline (proposal → spec → criteria → rules → review → tasks → execute). Each step reads files produced by its predecessor and makes no sense in isolation — a textbook Rule 4 bundle. Total content is ~1040 lines of substantive, language-agnostic guidance: EARS-based acceptance criteria with B-N → AC-N → RULE-N traceability, an Ecosystem Survey gate for technology decisions, a severity-tagged cross-document review step that produces a PASS/FAIL verdict gating the task step, YAML task output with dependency graph and checkpoints, and a checkpoint-halting execution loop. No JVM-specific content, but the Rule 5 escape applies — this is a coherent, high-quality workflow bundle, not a thin single step. The host repo (junie-midi-javafx-app, 0 stars) is an app project, but Rule 1 bars that as a reject signal; the skills make zero reference to JavaFX or MIDI and are fully reusable.
repo: JohannesRabauer/junie-midi-javafx-app
skill_path: ".agents/skills"
category: workflow
languages:
  - agnostic
trust: curated
author: Johannes Rabauer
version: "1.0.0"
last_updated: "2026-06-28"
scope: bundle
skills:
  - ".agents/skills/01-spec/SKILL.md"
  - ".agents/skills/02-criteria/SKILL.md"
  - ".agents/skills/03-rules/SKILL.md"
  - ".agents/skills/04-review/SKILL.md"
  - ".agents/skills/05-tasks/SKILL.md"
  - ".agents/skills/06-execute/SKILL.md"

reviewing-oracle-to-postgres-migrationanishi1222/multi-agent-code-reviewer :: reviewing-oracle-to-postgres-migrationAkihiro Nishikawa167-line database migration guide with real workflows (risk assessment, post-migration validation). Skill is transferable to any language/framework; does not assume Java. Per rule 69 (DPL 2026), JVM-fit must be the subject, not an accident—this could be applied verbatim to Python or JavaScript database work. Classified as needs_review because it's real and solid but off-topic-tech for JVM skills.
📋 yaml
name: Reviewing Oracle To Postgres Migration
description: >-
  Oracle-to-PostgreSQL migration risks guide: planning workflow, validation workflow, behavioral differences (empty strings, refcursors, type coercion, sorting, timestamps, transactions). Concrete, reusable but not JVM-specific.
repo: anishi1222/multi-agent-code-reviewer

showtailTingsters/Showtail :: showtailCassandra Chin088-line skill for Showtail (student work capture tool). Teaches how to set up tracking, check capture mode, generate reports. Real workflow guidance but not JVM-specific; applies equally to any language project. Per rule 5, a single non-JVM workflow skill does not qualify for escape clause—needs to be bundled with other workflow skills to elevate to found. Classified as needs_review because the skill is genuine and solid but off-topic for JVM directory.
📋 yaml
name: Showtail
description: >-
  Showtail skill for capturing student work trails (prompts and file changes). Hands-free tracking via hooks, session management, report generation. Real, reusable educational workflow tool.
repo: Tingsters/Showtail
skill_path: "assets/claude-code/plugin/skills/showtail/SKILL.md"

trust: curated

AGENTS.mduta8a/curatedz :: AGENTS.mdMasanori Tani095-line hybrid AGENTS.md mixing project-specific scaffolding (infrastructure, S3 layout, GitHub output format) with some architectural patterns (RSS pipeline, Step Functions orchestration, Rust Lambda + TypeScript CDK split). Per rule 70 (DPL 2026), hybrid project-docs with embedded skill content remain project-doc unless the transferable guidance is self-contained and primary. Here, guidance is heavily tied to this repo's AWS infrastructure decisions and S3 bucket structure. Classified as needs_review rather than reject because there are genuine patterns (pipeline orchestration, cost-focused choices) that could be reusable, but they are not sufficiently extracted or general.
📋 yaml
name: Agents
description: >-
  Repository guidelines for RSS→S3→GitHub Discussion pipeline on AWS. Architecture constraints (Lambda/Rust, Step Functions, S3 layout, GitHub output, Feedback forms), coding style (Rust, TypeScript CDK), testing, security/IAM. Mixed project scaffolding and architectural pattern.
repo: uta8a/curatedz
skill_path: "AGENTS.md"
category: project-doc
languages:
  - java
  - kotlin
trust: curated
author: Masanori Tani
version: "1.0.0"

enonic-app-managerwebmaxru/enonic-agent-skills :: enonic-app-managerMaxim Salnikov0Real procedural skill for Enonic XP (Java-based headless CMS) CLI commands and local development. Enonic XP is Java-based but niche in the JVM ecosystem compared to Spring/Quarkus. Substantive guidance (procedures, CLI patterns) but specialized platform. Fits JVM technically but outside mainstream Devoxx conference focus (Spring, Quarkus, microservices, cloud-native patterns).

name: Enonic App Manager
description: >-
  Real procedural skill for Enonic XP (Java-based headless CMS) CLI commands and local development. Enonic XP is Java-based but niche in the JVM ecosystem compared to Spring/Quarkus. Genuine JVM content but questionable reusability outside conference/talk context.
📋 yaml
name: Green Java Pr Report
description: >-
  Teaches transferable skill for auditing Java code performance and presenting examples with upstream evidence, but is thin (113 lines) and demo-coupled with hardcoded conference-specific paths. Genuine JVM content but questionable reusability outside conference/talk context.

okafka-java-codeanders-swanson/oracle-database-code-samples :: okafka-java-codeAnders Swanson38Teaches Java development with Oracle Kafka APIs through OKafka, including producer/consumer setup, OSON JSON serialization, Testcontainers testing, and transactional patterns. High JVM fit (Oracle Database, Kafka, Testcontainers). Thin at 46 lines and specialized to OKafka—a niche topic—but contains actionable implementation guidance. Worthy of review but may need depth validation.
📋 yaml
name: Okafka Java Code
description: >-
  Real JVM skill on Oracle Kafka OKafka APIs for Java. Covers OKafka producer/consumer patterns, OSON serialization, Testcontainers validation, authentication/properties, transactional flows, Oracle AI Database integration. Substantive with concrete implementation rules. Thin at 46 lines; references external reference files for deeper patterns. Specialized to OKafka (niche topic). Borderline between found and needs_review; marking needs_review due to brevity and specialization.
repo: anders-swanson/oracle-database-code-samples
skill_path: "skills/okafka-java-code/SKILL.md"
category: framework
languages:
  - java
  - kotlin
trust: curated
author: Anders Swanson
version: "1.0.0"
last_updated: "2026-06-28"

CLAUDE.mdascheman/jqa-eclipse-osgi-plugin :: CLAUDE.mdGerd Aschemann0CLAUDE.md mixes project-specific setup (Maven build, directory structure) with reusable jQAssistant plugin patterns (Cypher concepts, constraints, graph model design). The skill is OSGi/Eclipse analysis via jQA—clearly JVM-fit—but the transferability is reduced by repo-specific build guidance. Borderline project-doc with embedded skill patterns.

name: Claude
description: >-

CLAUDE.mddebe/adk-libpetri :: CLAUDE.mdDennis Berger1Adapts Google ADK orchestration core to use libpetri Petri Net runtime. Covers 7 stock subnets, PetriAgent adapter, and EventStore observability. Depth comparable to libpetri but this is an application/integration layer of the canonical libpetri (debe/libpetri, 18 stars). Canonical multi-language library is the promoted entry; this application variant is promoted-worthy but secondary.
📋 yaml
name: Claude
description: >-
  Adapts Google ADK orchestration core to use libpetri Petri Net runtime. Covers 7 stock subnets, PetriAgent adapter, and EventStore observability. Depth comparable to libpetri but this is an application/integration layer of the canonical libpetri (debe/libpetri, 18 stars). Canonical multi-language library is the promoted entry; this application variant is promoted-worthy but secondary.

review-paneljohnmeunier/zava-worshop-retry :: review-panelJohnathan MEUNIER0Multi-persona expert advisory review skill with fan-out + synthesizer pattern. Three specialist agents (correctness, security, test-coverage) run in parallel, schema-validate returns, synthesize stance. Depth 2 (185 lines, full topology + execution steps). 🔍 Needs human classification · 194 · the promotion queue

Real SKILL.md files the auto-classifier could not judge JVM-vs-offtopic from the path. Read these and promote the JVM ones to skills/*.yaml.
SkillRepoAuthorReasoning
playwright-javalordofthejars/test-codeassistants-workshop :: playwright-javaAlex SotoOpus recheck rejected promotion: SKILL.md explicitly states it was adapted from sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills — vendored/copied content, not lordofthejars's own work; also contains non-JVM AGI boilerplate (Python scripts, npm agi-agent-kit) appended as a template.. (judge: 428-line skill with concrete, opinionated guidance on production-quality test automation. JVM-focused (JUnit 5, Playwright Java, Allure), depth 3. Demo repo doesn't diminish quality.)
buildlordofthejars/hello-world-tekton-bob :: buildAlex SotoOpus recheck rejected promotion: Primarily a Tekton/Kubernetes container orchestration skill; Maven is only one of three steps (git-clone, mvn build, buildah push) — JVM tooling is incidental, not the skill's focus, so insufficiently JVM-specific for this collection.. (judge: 79-line Tekton build skill using Maven. JVM medium fit (Maven is JVM tool but skill emphasizes container orchestration). Numbered step in bundle (rule 4 applies); evaluated as standalone step with real build guidance.)
pipelinelordofthejars/hello-world-tekton-bob :: pipelineAlex SotoOpus recheck rejected promotion: Generic Tekton/Kubernetes pipeline generation with no JVM-specific content — references only Dockerfile and generic build/security tasks, nothing Java/Kotlin/Gradle/Maven-specific; thin demo-repo step.. (judge: 39 lines, depth 1. Generic Kubernetes orchestration, not JVM-specific. Bundle rule 4 applies but this individual step is thin (single numbered step without substantial independent guidance).)
secretslordofthejars/hello-world-tekton-bob :: secretsAlex SotoOpus recheck rejected promotion: Generic Kubernetes Secret/ServiceAccount generation — no JVM specificity, thin YAML-template guidance, and a numbered bundle step in a Tekton demo repo rather than a standalone reusable skill.. (judge: 52-line Kubernetes infrastructure step. Low JVM fit (Secret/ServiceAccount management). Thin guidance, numbered bundle step without substantial independent reusability.)
securitylordofthejars/hello-world-tekton-bob :: securityAlex SotoOpus recheck rejected promotion: Not JVM-specific; thin Tekton/Trivy YAML boilerplate with no transferable JVM guidance — just a single-step prompt to generate a Kubernetes Task definition.. (judge: 46-line vulnerability scanning skill using Trivy. Low JVM fit. Thin numbered step in bundle, single-purpose without substantial transferable guidance.)
neitnataztech910/NEITN :: neitnAndrei TazetdinovOpus recheck rejected promotion: Skill is entirely neitn-product-specific (workflow DSL, Patch Schema v0, flow.yaml) with zero JVM relevance; not reusable outside that proprietary tool.. (judge: 55-line skill for workflow DSL (Patch Schema v0). Low JVM fit (generic workflow tool). Real guidance but narrow domain, thin reusability outside neitn context.)
saku-integrationasierzapata/saku :: saku-integrationAsier ZapataOpus recheck rejected promotion: Genuine reusable agent skill for the saku/tdo Rust CLI task manager, but has zero JVM connection — not JVM-specific.. (judge: 154-line task manager skill. Real guidance but low JVM fit (generic task tool). Narrow tool domain but sufficient depth for needs_review.)
check-elasticsearchdadoonet/fscrawler :: check-elasticsearchDavid PilatoOpus recheck rejected promotion: Skill uses generic curl-based Elasticsearch REST API calls with no JVM-specific tooling; it is stack-agnostic (works in any language), and the 3-bullet instruction set is too thin to constitute a reusable skill.. (judge: 22-line Elasticsearch operations skill. High JVM fit (Elasticsearch, Java-native). Thin but focused; reusable for Elasticsearch diagnostics in Java projects. Narrow scope but genuine skill.)
start-elasticsearchdadoonet/fscrawler :: start-elasticsearchDavid PilatoOpus recheck rejected promotion: Skill is project-specific (hardcodes fscrawler's IGNORE_ME directory) and trivially thin — just a curl command to start ES; the JVM connection is indirect and it does not generalize cleanly to other JVM projects without modification.. (judge: 28-line ES startup skill. High JVM fit (Elasticsearch Java runtime). Thin but operational—reusable for local ES setup in JVM testing.)
agents.mdkdubois/kubernetes-aiops-agent :: agents.mdKevin DuboisOpus recheck rejected promotion: agents.md is a project-specific AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md-style AI assistant instructions file for this single repo; the LangChain4j/Quarkus pattern snippets are minimal examples embedded in project setup docs, not a standalone reusable skill.. (judge: 206-line Quarkus development guide with LangChain4j, multi-agent patterns, Kubernetes tooling. High JVM fit (Java 21, Quarkus, LangChain4j). Depth 2 development guidance, reusable patterns for agentic JVM systems.)
AGENTS.mdkdubois/netatmo-java-mcp :: AGENTS.mdKevin DuboisOpus recheck rejected promotion: AGENTS.md is a project-specific operation guide for the netatmo-java-mcp app (build commands, env vars, architecture map, Kubernetes deployment), not a reusable JVM skill — it documents how to work on this one application, not a generalizable Quarkus/MCP pattern transferable to other projects.. (judge: 123-line Quarkus MCP server skill covering REST API design, OAuth2, streaming, native builds. High JVM fit (Quarkus, Java MCP). Depth 2 with architectural guidance on services and transport layers.)
mcp-addpaoloricciuti/mcp-add :: mcp-addPaolo RicciutiOpus recheck rejected promotion: Generic npx/Node.js MCP-server configuration tool with zero JVM content — fails JVM-specificity requirement entirely.. (judge: 80-line MCP configuration skill. Low JVM fit (generic protocol setup). Real reusable guidance but not JVM-specific. Needs review: genuine skill but off-topic-tech domain.)

Would require significant refactoring for reuse at other conferences. Depth 1: functional but narrow scope.)
tdd-disciplinetpierrain/use-case-driven-harness :: tdd-disciplineThomas PIERRAINOpus recheck rejected promotion: Explicitly language-agnostic TDD discipline reference — not JVM-specific; also only 45 lines, well below depth threshold for a foundational skill.. (judge: Real skill with opinionated TDD guidance (baby steps, fail-first, triangulation, refactoring never optional), language-agnostic and foundational. However, at 45 lines it falls below the ~100+ line threshold for 'found' and reads as a concise discipline reference rather than a deep guide. Borderline between slim foundational wisdom and needs_review due to brevity relative to depth expectation.)
the-hive-patterntpierrain/use-case-driven-harness :: the-hive-patternThomas PIERRAINOpus recheck rejected promotion: All code examples are C#/.NET (IBookingApi, sealed class, Task<Result<T>>, [ApiController]); Java mentioned only in passing — not a JVM skill despite language-agnostic framing.. (judge: Comprehensive 319-line architecture skill describing The Hive pattern — modular monolith design with vertical slicing, hexagonal module boundaries, ports/adapters between modules, and network-aware design. Language-agnostic (examples in C#/.NET but explicitly transferable to Java, TypeScript) with deep structural guidance. Strong depth, concrete opinionated patterns, and substantial guidance on backend organization.)
CLAUDE.mdtsundberg/scheme-by-claude :: CLAUDE.mdThomas SundbergOpus recheck rejected promotion: Bare project-doc: CLAUDE.md is an autonomous-coding experiment prompt specific to one Scheme-interpreter project (hardcoded package, file paths, branch naming), not a reusable JVM skill applicable across projects.. (judge: Comprehensive 336-line guide to implementing an R7RS Scheme interpreter in Java via strict TDD and Cucumber-based conformance testing. Provides concrete opinionated structure: extract examples from R7RS PDF into Cucumber scenarios chapter-by-chapter, organize feature files by standard sections, implement additional programs (Tower of Hanoi, Fibonacci). Language-specific (Java) but with deep, actionable guidance on standards-driven implementation. High JVM fit.)

CLAUDE.mdascheman/jqa-dashboard-plugin :: CLAUDE.mdGerd AschemannOpus recheck rejected promotion: CLAUDE.md is a project context doc, not a reusable skill: all 'patterns' are listed only as project-specific file paths with no extracted, transferable knowledge; the referenced 'Cypress editor abstraction' does not exist (it is a Cypher/CodeMirror editor, not Cypress), undermining the claim.. (judge: CLAUDE.md documents a hybrid jQAssistant plugin + React dashboard project. Contains reusable patterns (Neo4j driver lifecycle, React hook design, Cypress editor abstraction) alongside project-specific paths and Maven setup. The transferable skill (how to structure a plugin+dashboard combo) exists but is mixed with this repo's layout. Borderline.)
greengtrefs/tdd-pbt-plugin :: greenGregor TrefsOpus recheck rejected promotion: Skill is explicitly dual-language (Java+TypeScript equal first-class options), repo primary language is Shell — not JVM-specific enough for a jvm-skills collection.. (judge: Part of comprehensive TDD/PBT workflow bundle. Green phase (implement minimal code to pass failing test) is a core step of the TDD cycle. Interconnected with red, refactor, and property phases. High-quality, dual-language (Java/TypeScript) teaching pipeline.)
test-listgtrefs/tdd-pbt-plugin :: test-listGregor TrefsOpus recheck rejected promotion: Repo belongs to 'gtrefs', not the target user (tschuehly); and the skill is explicitly multi-language (Java+jqwik AND TypeScript+fast-check), failing both the ownership and JVM-specificity requirements.. (judge: First phase of a comprehensive numbered TDD/PBT workflow bundle (test-list → red → green → refactor, plus property-first variants + tutorial). Coherent, high-quality pipeline with clear handoffs; supports Java (jqwik) and TypeScript (fast-check). Per Rule 5, workflow bundles of this quality and coherence are directory-worthy despite not being JVM-specific. The bundle teaches disciplined development practice applicable across languages.)
AGENTS.mdjetztgradnet/java-midi-workshop :: AGENTS.mdWolfgang SchellOpus recheck rejected promotion: AGENTS.md is project-context documentation (package names, Gradle tasks, file paths, known gaps) for a specific workshop; embedded MIDI API patterns are secondary and inseparable from project-specific content, failing the reusable-skill bar.. (judge: Workshop project AGENTS.md (146 lines) mixing project layout and build setup (Gradle, Java 21, package structure) with reusable patterns (DeviceUtil helper design, standalone example pattern, GM patch filtering logic, MIDI API patterns). The transferable skill (how to structure hands-on Java MIDI examples for teaching) is embedded but secondary to workshop workflow documentation. Per Rule 3, hybrid project-doc with skill patterns; borderline.)

spark-engineerstephanj/claude-code-collections :: spark-engineerStephan JanssenOpus recheck rejected promotion: Skill explicitly covers PySpark (Python) alongside Scala/Java — triggers include PySpark, output templates say 'PySpark or Scala', related skills link to Python Pro for PySpark; the content is deliberately language-agnostic with no JVM-specific guidance (no Scala Dataset API, no JVM GC/heap tuning), so it does not qualify as a JVM-specific skill.. Three specialist agents (correctness, security, test-coverage) run in parallel, schema-validate returns, synthesize stance. Depth 2 (185 lines, full topology + execution steps). High-quality workflow but off-topic (generic agentic SDLC review, not JVM). Part of Agentic SDLC track curriculum.)

java-docsanishi1222/multi-agent-code-reviewer :: java-docsAkihiro NishikawaOpus recheck rejected promotion: 23-line Javadoc tag checklist in a multi-agent demo repo — content is generic boilerplate (standard tag listing) with no unique depth, examples, or JVM-specific insight beyond official Java documentation; serves as a demo fixture rather than a genuinely reusable skill.. (judge: 23-line Javadoc guidance is narrow and modest in depth, but covers genuine Java documentation practice. Borderline thin; qualifies as needs_review rather than reject because the guidance is specific to Java and transferable.)
reviewing-oracle-to-postgres-migrationanishi1222/multi-agent-code-reviewer :: reviewing-oracle-to-postgres-migrationAkihiro NishikawaOpus recheck rejected promotion: Language-agnostic Oracle-to-PostgreSQL migration workflow; no JVM/Java/JDBC/Hibernate specifics — applies verbatim to Python or Node.js database work, failing rule 69 JVM-fit requirement.. (judge: 67-line database migration guide with real workflows (risk assessment, post-migration validation). Skill is transferable to any language/framework; does not assume Java. Per rule 69 (DPL 2026), JVM-fit must be the subject, not an accident—this could be applied verbatim to Python or JavaScript database work. Classified as needs_review because it's real and solid but off-topic-tech for JVM skills.)
showtailTingsters/Showtail :: showtailCassandra ChinOpus recheck rejected promotion: Genuine skill but entirely language-agnostic (student work capture via Showtail CLI); zero JVM-specific content, fails the JVM-specific requirement.. (judge: 88-line skill for Showtail (student work capture tool). Teaches how to set up tracking, check capture mode, generate reports. Real workflow guidance but not JVM-specific; applies equally to any language project. Per rule 5, a single non-JVM workflow skill does not qualify for escape clause—needs to be bundled with other workflow skills to elevate to found. Classified as needs_review because the skill is genuine and solid but off-topic for JVM directory.)
cdk-list-resourcesuta8a/playground :: cdk-list-resourcesMasanori TaniOpus recheck rejected promotion: AWS CDK resource enumeration skill using `npx cdk synth`; entirely AWS/CloudFormation-focused with no JVM-specific content — applies equally to TypeScript, JavaScript, or Python CDK projects.. (judge: 53-line AWS CDK resource enumeration skill. Shows how to run cdk synth, parse CloudFormation, extract resources by service. Solid guidance but entirely AWS/CloudFormation-focused; would apply equally to JavaScript/TypeScript or Python CDK projects. Not JVM-relevant; classified as needs_review.)
commit-messageuta8a/playground :: commit-messageMasanori TaniOpus recheck rejected promotion: Generic Git commit-message formatter with zero JVM-specific content; off-topic workflow skill that does not bundle any JVM tooling.. (judge: 61-line generic Git commit message formatting skill. Teaches format detection, Conventional Commits, subject/body/footer structure. Reusable across any project/language but not JVM-specific. Per rule 5, single workflow skill does not escape the off-topic-workflow classification without bundling. Classified as needs_review because guidance is solid but off-topic.)
AGENTS.mduta8a/curatedz :: AGENTS.mdMasanori TaniOpus recheck rejected promotion: Pure project-doc: Rust Lambda + TypeScript CDK stack (not JVM), all guidance is repo-specific (this project's S3 layout, GitHub Discussion output format, HMAC feedback form, specific IAM policy), no transferable JVM skill content.. (judge: 95-line hybrid AGENTS.md mixing project-specific scaffolding (infrastructure, S3 layout, GitHub output format) with some architectural patterns (RSS pipeline, Step Functions orchestration, Rust Lambda + TypeScript CDK split). Per rule 70 (DPL 2026), hybrid project-docs with embedded skill content remain project-doc unless the transferable guidance is self-contained and primary. Here, guidance is heavily tied to this repo's AWS infrastructure decisions and S3 bucket structure. Classified as needs_review rather than reject because there are genuine patterns (pipeline orchestration, cost-focused choices) that *could* be reusable, but they are not sufficiently extracted or general.)
enable-prompt-cachingjbaruch/koog-tessl :: enable-prompt-cachingBaruch SadogurskyOpus recheck rejected promotion: Bulk of the 64-line skill explains Anthropic API mechanics (billing, TTL, token minimums) rather than JVM/Koog-specific knowledge; the only JVM-specific content is a ~10-line Kotlin snippet using CacheControl.Ephemeral, which is too thin to justify inclusion given the repo has 0 stars and no description.. (judge: Teaches Anthropic prompt caching configuration in Koog 1.0. Thin (64 lines), provider-specific feature; valuable but borderline on whether the caching concept is JVM-fit or just happens to live in Koog. Recommend for inclusion but marked needs_review due to provider-specificity.)

use-analysisfranz1981/ClaudeFranzBFF :: use-analysisFrancesco NigroOpus recheck rejected promotion: Reusability limited to perf-lab log format; JVM-specificity is incidental (analyzes JVM processes via vmstat/mpstat/pidstat) rather than JVM-native (GC, JIT, heap, threads) — the core USE method is generic Linux system analysis that happens to target JVM processes.. (judge: Real performance analysis methodology (USE method) applied to JVM benchmarking context. Content shows understanding of system metrics (CPU utilization, saturation, errors), concrete column positions in performance tools, and perf-lab result interpretation. JVM fit is medium: perf-lab is JVM-specific, but vmstat/pidstat are generic system tools. Depth is solid (252 lines with structured steps) but scope is narrow: benchmark result analysis rather than general JVM performance diagnosis. Candidate for promotion if the scope can extend beyond perf-lab, or if perf-lab is the canonical benchmark framework for JVM performance work in the community. Needs review to clarify whether benchmark-specific guidance meets 'reusable skill' bar.)

action-skillArturSkowronski/clawd-reachy-mini :: action-skillArtur SkowronskitechReachy Mini robot control skill: move head/antennas, emotions, camera, TTS. Concrete tools (75 lines, depth 2). Not JVM; is robotics hardware integration, not JVM target.
AGENTS.mdChristophHaag/vibecoded-ubuntu-touch-geoclue-bridge :: AGENTS.mdChristoph Haagtech103-line D-Bus/Python bridge for Ubuntu Touch geolocation. Technical depth but entirely Linux systems-level programming (D-Bus protocol, GeoClue2 service bridging), not JVM-related. Could be lifted to any Linux system without change.
AGENTS.mdChristophHaag/vibecoded-ubuntu-touch-xwayland-position-fix :: AGENTS.mdChristoph Haagtech75-line X11/Xwayland input coordinate clamping fix for Ubuntu Touch. Linux systems programming (Wayland/X11 interop), not JVM.
AGENTS.mdChristophHaag/vibecoding-tests :: AGENTS.mdChristoph Haagtech150-line C++/CMake workspace guide for OpenXR/Monado test suite. Monado is OpenXR runtime (C++), completely non-JVM. Project layout guidance specific to C++ multi-project setup.
debug_layerChristophHaag/vibecoding-tests :: debug_layerChristoph Haagtech129-line C++/OpenXR debug layer guide. OpenXR API layer interception patterns, X-macro dispatch tables — pure C++ systems programming. No JVM applicability.

phpstan-custom-ruleDaveLiddament/php-claude-skills :: phpstan-custom-ruleDave LiddamenttechPHPStan custom rule skill (349 lines). Off-topic tech (PHP static analysis); not JVM-applicable.

AsyncApiSkillGrinseteddy/SamplesDddMeetAi :: AsyncApiSkillAnnegret JunkertechAsyncAPI 3.1.0 specification author skill: 177 lines of guidance on writing event-driven API specs in YAML (Kafka, MQTT, AMQP, WebSocket). While applicable to JVM projects, the skill itself is generic API design (no JVM-specific patterns or libraries). Topic is message-broker API design, not JVM. Reject as off-topic-tech.
OpenApiSkillGrinseteddy/SamplesDddMeetAi :: OpenApiSkillAnnegret JunkertechOpenAPI 3.1.0 specification author skill: 142 lines of guidance on writing REST API contracts in YAML (resources, endpoints, auth, versioning). Generic API design methodology, not JVM-specific. Topic is REST API spec authoring. Reject as off-topic-tech.
asyncapi-spec-authorGrinseteddy/DomainDrivenApiDesign :: asyncapi-spec-authorAnnegret JunkertechDuplicate of SamplesDddMeetAi asyncapi-spec-author: 177 lines on AsyncAPI 3.1.0 YAML specification writing (Kafka, MQTT, AMQP). Generic async API design, not JVM-specific. Per rule 2, same skill appears in multiple repos; canonical repo (DomainDrivenApiDesign has 6 stars vs SamplesDddMeetAi's 1) would be promoted once. Both reject for off-topic-tech.
openapi-spec-authorGrinseteddy/DomainDrivenApiDesign :: openapi-spec-authorAnnegret JunkertechDuplicate of SamplesDddMeetAi openapi-spec-author: 142 lines on OpenAPI 3.1.0 YAML specification writing (resources, endpoints, auth). Generic REST API design, not JVM. Per rule 2 single-promotion principle, both reject as off-topic-tech.
Domain Storytelling OpenAPIGrinseteddy/AiCollections :: Domain Storytelling OpenAPIAnnegret Junkertech709-line OpenAPI skill combining Domain Storytelling notation with OpenAPI spec generation. While large, the focus is API specification (off-topic-tech), not JVM. The bundled Domain Storytelling guidance is generic and the primary deliverable is OpenAPI YAML. Reject as off-topic-tech.

safe-frontend-map-list-searchJohannesRabauer/lesungen-deutschland :: safe-frontend-map-list-searchJohannes Rabauertech41-line skill about React/TypeScript frontend changes (App.tsx, MapComponent, Leaflet). React is not JVM tech. Could lift verbatim into a non-JVM project (e.g., Node/Vue). Per Devoxx Poland learnings: if a skill's subject is not fundamentally JVM, classify it as off-topic-tech, not project-doc — this is a frontend React skill that happens to live in a JVM-adjacent repo.

Substantive and transferable workflow principles, but language-agnostic and not JVM-specific. Per rules, generic reusable workflow = off-topic-workflow.
code-reviewerAdamBien/zsmith :: code-reviewerAdam BienworkflowCode review workflow (naming, complexity) — generic development practice, not JVM-specific. Could apply to Python, Rust, Go projects unchanged.
explainAdamBien/zsmith :: explainAdam BienworkflowConcept explanation workflow — generic communication technique not specific to JVM technologies.
bceAdamBien/airails :: bceAdam BienworkflowBoundary-Control-Entity (BCE) pattern — technology-neutral architecture pattern. Could be applied unchanged to Python, Go, or Node projects. JVM context is incidental, not essential.
continuous-testingAdamBien/airails :: continuous-testingAdam BienworkflowContinuous test loop workflow (build, unit tests, integration, system tests) — generic development practice applying equally to Python, Go, or Node projects with Quarkus-style dev servers.
sbceAdamBien/airails :: sbceAdam BienworkflowSpec-driven BCE workflow — architecture methodology independent of language. The spec-to-BC mapping and phase workflow apply equally to Python, Go, or JavaScript projects.
showtimeAdamBien/airails :: showtimeAdam BienworkflowLive coding mode modifier — workflow instruction to skip tests during demos. Generic development workflow not JVM-specific.
bce-diagramsAdamBien/airails :: bce-diagramsAdam BienworkflowArchitecture diagram generation workflow (Mermaid/draw.io for BCE) — generic documentation methodology not specific to JVM.
readmeAdamBien/airails :: readmeAdam BienworkflowREADME writing methodology for developers — generic documentation workflow not tied to JVM.
github-issue-publisherDanielFloor/DevDiary :: github-issue-publisherDaniël FloorworkflowSkill for publishing pre-approved GitHub issue drafts to the DevDiary repo using github/issue_write MCP. Handles label inference from issue content, directory renaming, and PR creation—a GitHub-centric workflow automation skill with no JVM connection. Generic developer workflow, not JVM-specific.
issue-draft-templateDanielFloor/DevDiary :: issue-draft-templateDaniël FloorworkflowSkill for drafting GitHub issues using the StudyBuddy+ User Story format. Teaches issue structure (user story, acceptance criteria, technical notes, task breakdown, stacked PR breakdown) and a quality checklist—a generic GitHub workflow pattern with no JVM specificity.
skill-creatorDanielFloor/DevDiary :: skill-creatorDaniël FloorworkflowMeta-skill for creating, editing, and evaluating other Claude Code skills. Teaches skill-writing patterns, evaluation setup, and performance optimization. While comprehensive (485 lines), this is about skill authorship itself, not a domain skill. No JVM relevance—applies equally to any skill domain.
interviewJohannesRabauer/java-project-analyser :: interviewJohannes Rabauerworkflow51-line generic agentic workflow skill for conducting structured interviews. No JVM content — could run in a Python or Node project identically. Interview methodology is generic dev workflow, not JVM-specific. Per Rule 5 learnings: generic agent workflows (code review, spec-driven dev, PR defense, interview protocol) that aren't deeply JVM-coupled are off-topic-workflow, regardless of repo context.
specJohannesRabauer/java-project-analyser :: specJohannes Rabauerworkflow55-line generic spec-generation workflow skill. Methodology (mine conversation + explore codebase + fill gaps + output markdown) is language/framework-agnostic. No JVM content. Per Rule 5: single well-developed non-JVM skill (even if 130 lines, high quality) is off-topic-workflow if its core discipline (spec writing, code review, interview) isn't JVM-tied. Workflow depth does not override category.
tdd-taskJohannesRabauer/java-project-analyser :: tdd-taskJohannes Rabauerworkflow149-line TDD workflow skill (red-green-refactor, test classification, task tracking). Generic agentic development methodology applicable to Python, Go, Node equally. Per Rule 5 learnings: workflow skills (even substantial ones with 9-step process, automation rules, test base class references) that aren't fundamentally JVM-constrained are off-topic-workflow. This teaches test discipline, not a JVM pattern.
ui-reviewJohannesRabauer/java-project-analyser :: ui-reviewJohannes Rabauerworkflow64-line generic UI review workflow (run Playwright tests, spawn subagent, review screenshots). Playwright is JS/browser automation, not JVM-specific. Workflow (generate screenshots → review → fix) is frontend-agnostic. Off-topic-workflow: generic UI QA process.
defend-your-prJohannesRabauer/junie-midi-javafx-app :: defend-your-prJohannes Rabauerworkflow132-line PR review workflow (two-stage gate: comprehension interrogation then design review). While well-written and applicable to Java projects, the methodology (gated review, evidence-based questioning, design tradeoffs) is language/framework-agnostic. Per Devoxx Poland 2026 learnings: code-review workflow that happens to live in a Java repo is off-topic-workflow; the discipline itself isn't JVM-specific.

xavieratilafassina/xavier :: xavierAtila FassinaworkflowGeneric agent orchestration framework: routes commands to skills, resolves vault config, manages skill dependencies. No JVM content. The entire Xavier system (entries #22-39) teaches agentic workflows, agent management, skill composition—all applicable equally to Python/Node/Rust projects. Per Learnings (2026-06-27 Devoxx): 'JVM fit must be the subject, not an accident.' Xavier is subject-agnostic orchestration, not JVM-specific development.
multi-model-dispatchatilafassina/xavier :: multi-model-dispatchAtila FassinaworkflowInternal Xavier utility for dispatching prompts to external LLMs (GPT, Gemini, etc.) and parsing stream-json output. Generic agent/LLM orchestration pattern, no JVM content. Framework-agnostic.
add-depatilafassina/xavier :: add-depAtila FassinaworkflowCreates knowledge notes for Node.js packages—not applicable to JVM projects. Xavier is a Node-centric agent system (references package.json, npm, Node packages). Even as part of Xavier's coherent system, this is off-topic-workflow per Rule 5 learnings: single workflow/tool step without JVM signal.
askatilafassina/xavier :: askAtila FassinaworkflowGeneric code-question-answering workflow. While comprehensive (438 lines), it teaches how to orchestrate research, synthesize vault knowledge, and defer to background agents. No JVM guidance. deslopifyglaforge/glaforge.github.io :: deslopifyGuillaume Laforgeworkflowdeslopify skill (21 lines) teaches text rewriting to remove AI tropes. This is a generic LLM communication/writing style skill applicable to any project (Python, JS, Java, etc.), not JVM-specific. Rule 69: 'A skill that crawls an Angular UI with one incidental actuator curl, or a code-review workflow that happens to live in a Java repo, is not JVM-fit.' This text editing skill is not JVM even though it lives in a developer's GitHub Pages.
deslopifyglaforge/agentic-design-patterns :: deslopifyGuillaume Laforgeworkflowdeslopify skill (16 lines) in agentic-design-patterns repo; identical copy of deslopify (text rewriting, removes AI tropes). Generic writing style skill, not JVM-specific. Rule 2: 'A copied skill is promoted ONCE, at its canonical repo.' Canonical is the standalone deslopify repo; this is a duplicate.
deslopifyglaforge/deslopify :: deslopifyGuillaume Laforgeworkflowdeslopify SKILL.md (16 lines) in canonical deslopify repo; teaches text rewriting to remove AI tropes and clichés. Generic communication/writing style skill transferable to any language (Python, JS, Rust, Java, etc.), not JVM-specific. Rule 69: if skill could be lifted verbatim into a Python project without change, it is off-topic-workflow.

stackjavoire/stackinator :: stackJonatan Dahlworkflow40-line SKILL.md teaching the `stack` CLI for git stacked branch management: choosing between `stack worktree` vs `stack new`, common commands (new, status, show, sync, up, down, prune, reparent, rename), and workflow (create feature, commit, sync, check status, prune merged). This is real, coherent git workflow guidance and reusable, but it is not JVM-specific. Per evaluation rule 5, workflow skills require a coherent bundle to escape off-topic classification. A single SKILL.md file, even well-developed, is "a single thin workflow step" and falls under off-topic-workflow when not JVM.

landing-page-builderjlengrand/my-skills :: landing-page-builderjulien Lengrand-LambertworkflowSkill for building high-conversion landing pages. nvc-coachjlengrand/my-skills :: nvc-coachjulien Lengrand-LambertworkflowNon-Violent Communication coaching skill based on Marshall Rosenberg's framework. Teaches the four NVC components (observations, feelings, needs, requests) and four modes (situation coaching, message reformulation, retrospective reflection, concepts/techniques). Real skill (108 lines) for interpersonal communication, not JVM-related.
slc-scopejlengrand/my-skills :: slc-scopejulien Lengrand-LambertworkflowMVP scoping skill using the SLC (Simple, Lovable, Complete) framework. Teaches feature classification (KEEP/CUT/DEFER), three operation modes (scope from scratch, cut existing features, weekend build reality check). Real skill (119 lines) for product development, not JVM-specific.
validate-app-ideajlengrand/my-skills :: validate-app-ideajulien Lengrand-LambertworkflowApp idea validation skill using the painkiller/vitamin/candy framework. Teaches three validation questions (people searching, people paying, one-sentence clarity) and three operation modes (evaluate new idea, stress-test existing, compare two ideas). Real skill (108 lines) for product/business validation, not JVM development.
video-to-skillsjlengrand/my-skills :: video-to-skillsjulien Lengrand-LambertworkflowMeta skill for extracting reusable Claude Code skills from video transcripts. Teaches framework identification, skill candidate evaluation, skill file structure, and source attribution. blogpostkakkoyun/me :: blogpostKemal Akkoyunworkflow121-line blog post writing workflow skill. General-purpose authoring guide (audience, structure, tone), not JVM-specific.
the-unwindkakkoyun/me :: the-unwindKemal AkkoyunworkflowSubstantial, well-documented agentic workflow (321 lines) with clear steps for aggregating data sources and drafting newsletter issues. However, it is a personal content-management workflow tied to the author's specific tools (Readwise, Things3, recall CLI, personal vault) and production process. Not JVM-related, not framework-specific, and not transferable beyond this author's newsletter. Per Rule 5: a single well-developed non-JVM skill is `off-topic-workflow` regardless of depth or quality.

sdd-archivejoedayz/spec-driven-development :: sdd-archiveJosé DíazboilerplateDuplicate of sdd-yolo/.agents/skills/sdd-archive/SKILL.md (166 lines, identical content). This is a copy-paste of the workflow step into a tmp/taller training subdirectory. Per Rule 78 (Devoxx Poland 2026), once ≥3 identical files are confirmed across directories, all copies are boilerplate. The sdd-* skills appear in both sdd-yolo and sdd-yolo/tmp/taller directories.
sdd-featurejoedayz/spec-driven-development :: sdd-featureJosé DíazboilerplateDuplicate copy of sdd-feature (102 lines) in tmp/taller training directory. Boilerplate per Rule 78.

tallerjoedayz/spec-driven-development :: tallerJosé DíazboilerplateDuplicate copy of sdd-yolo/AGENTS.md (21 lines) in tmp/taller. Boilerplate per Rule 78.
CLAUDE.mdmarkpollack/agent-experiment-template :: CLAUDE.mdMark Pollackboilerplate76-line CLAUDE.md for agent experiment template scaffolding. Documents pre-wired experiment loop infrastructure, customization points (3 pluggable pieces), directory structure. Meta-guidance for using/customizing a template, not a domain skill. Per rule, template/boilerplate documentation = boilerplate.

templateswebmaxru/is-ai-native :: templatesMaxim SalnikovboilerplateEmpty skill template file in .squad/templates/. Duplicated boilerplate placeholder for copying into new skill definitions. Not a reusable skill, not JVM-related.
agent-collaborationwebmaxru/is-ai-native :: agent-collaborationMaxim SalnikovboilerplateTeam coordination patterns (worktree awareness, decision recording, cross-agent communication) in .squad/templates/skills/. This is a duplicated template file meant to be copied into projects. Workflow meta-skill, not JVM.
agent-conductwebmaxru/is-ai-native :: agent-conductMaxim SalnikovboilerplateHard rules for squad agents (product isolation, peer quality checks) in .squad/templates/skills/. Boilerplate template file duplicated across repositories. Team governance meta-pattern, not JVM skill.

appinsights-instrumentationwebmaxru/octocat_supply :: appinsights-instrumentationMaxim SalnikovboilerplateSkill file appinsights-instrumentation copied from webmaxru/copilot_agent_mode into octocat_supply demo repo. Per Rule 3 (hybrid docs), the scaffolding content (module-a/b/c/d, Docker setup, specific ports) is project-bound; transferable guidance requires extraction and is not self-contained in this file.

CLAUDE.mdtpierrain/SmokeMe :: CLAUDE.mdThomas PIERRAINproject-docThis CLAUDE.md is project-specific scaffolding: build/test commands, architecture walkthrough for SmokeMe's components (SmokeTestAutoFinder, SmokeTestRunner, AspNetCore adapters), configuration keys (appsettings.json), solution structure, testing conventions, and CI workflow. No transferable skill guidance — documentation for THIS library's internals, not reusable patterns that can be extracted and applied elsewhere.
CLAUDE.mdtpierrain/Diverse :: CLAUDE.mdThomas PIERRAINproject-docHybrid project documentation: mixes library-specific architectural guidance (sub-fuzzers, NoDuplication memoizer, determinism contract via fixed seed indexing) with project conventions (don't modify static data, use IFuzz, extension methods). While determinism and NoDuplication patterns are interesting, they are tightly coupled to Diverse's implementation. The critical guidance (static data immutability for reproducibility, seeding behavior) cannot be extracted unchanged into a different fuzzing library — it is project-doc per rule 2026-06-27.
